CHIP Eligibility for Children Ineligible for Medicaid as a Result of Elimination of Disregards, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–148, title II, § 2101(f)124 Stat. 287Mar. 23, 2010“Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a State shall treat any child who is determined to be ineligible for medical assistance under the State Medicaid plan or under a waiver of the plan as a result of the elimination of the application of an income disregard based on expense or type of income, as required under section 1902(e)(14) of the Social Security Act [] (as added by this Act), as a targeted low-income child under section 2110(b) [] (unless the child is excluded under paragraph (2) of that section) and shall provide child health assistance to the child under the State child health plan (whether implemented under title XIX or XXI, or both, of the Social Security Act [ et seq., 1397aa et seq.]).”42 U.S.C. 1396a(e)(14)42 U.S.C. 1397jj(b)42 U.S.C. 1396

CHIP Eligibility for Children Ineligible for Medicaid as a Result of Elimination of Disregards, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–148, title II, § 2101(f)124 Stat. 287Mar. 23, 2010“Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a State shall treat any child who is determined to be ineligible for medical assistance under the State Medicaid plan or under a waiver of the plan as a result of the elimination of the application of an income disregard based on expense or type of income, as required under section 1902(e)(14) of the Social Security Act [] (as added by this Act), as a targeted low-income child under section 2110(b) [] (unless the child is excluded under paragraph (2) of that section) and shall provide child health assistance to the child under the State child health plan (whether implemented under title XIX or XXI, or both, of the Social Security Act [ et seq., 1397aa et seq.]).”42 U.S.C. 1396a(e)(14)42 U.S.C. 1397jj(b)42 U.S.C. 1396

children.text: Inpatient hospital services.
Outpatient hospital services.
Physician services.
Surgical services.
Clinic services (including health center services) and other ambulatory health care services.

Prescription drugs and biologicals and the administration of such drugs and biologicals, only if such drugs and biologicals are not furnished for the purpose of causing, or assisting in causing, the death, suicide, euthanasia, or mercy killing of a person.
Over-the-counter medications.
Laboratory and radiological services.
Prenatal care and prepregnancy family planning services and supplies.

Inpatient mental health services, other than services described in paragraph (18) but including services furnished in a State-operated mental hospital and including residential or other 24-hour therapeutically planned structured services.
Outpatient mental health services, other than services described in paragraph (19) but including services furnished in a State-operated mental hospital and including community-based services.
Durable medical equipment and other medically-related or remedial devices (such as prosthetic devices, implants, eyeglasses, hearing aids, dental devices, and adaptive devices).
Disposable medical supplies.

Home and community-based health care services and related supportive services (such as home health nursing services, home health aide services, personal care, assistance with activities of daily living, chore services, day care services, respite care services, training for family members, and minor modifications to the home).

Nursing care services (such as nurse practitioner services, nurse midwife services, advanced practice nurse services, private duty nursing care, pediatric nurse services, and respiratory care services) in a home, school, or other setting.
Abortion only if necessary to save the life of the mother or if the pregnancy is the result of an act of rape or incest.
Dental services.
Inpatient substance use treatment services and residential substance use treatment services.
Outpatient substance use treatment services.
Case management services.
Care coordination services.
Physical therapy, occupational therapy, and services for individuals with speech, hearing, and language disorders.

Hospice care (concurrent, in the case of an individual who is a child, with care related to the treatment of the child’s condition with respect to which a diagnosis of terminal illness has been made.1So in original. A closing parenthesis probably should precede the period.1

children.label: A
children.text: prescribed by or furnished by a physician or other licensed or registered practitioner within the scope of practice as defined by State law,

children.label: B
children.text: performed under the general supervision or at the direction of a physician, or

children.label: C
children.text: furnished by a health care facility that is operated by a State or local government or is licensed under State law and operating within the scope of the license.
Premiums for private health care insurance coverage.
Medical transportation.

Enabling services (such as transportation, translation, and outreach services) only if designed to increase the accessibility of primary and preventive health care services for eligible low-income individuals.
Any other health care services or items specified by the Secretary and not excluded under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: who has been determined eligible by the State for child health assistance under the State plan;

children.label: i
children.text: who is a low-income child, or

children.label: I
children.text: whose family income (as determined under the State child health plan) exceeds the medicaid applicable income level (as defined in paragraph (4)), but does not exceed 50 percentage points above the medicaid applicable income level;

children.label: II
children.text: whose family income (as so determined) does not exceed the medicaid applicable income level (as defined in paragraph (4) but determined as if “” were substituted for “”); orJune 1, 1997March 31, 1997

children.label: III
children.text: who resides in a State that does not have a medicaid applicable income level (as defined in paragraph (4)); and

children.label: C
children.text: who is not found to be eligible for medical assistance under subchapter XIX or, subject to paragraph (5), covered under a group health plan or under health insurance coverage (as such terms are defined in ).section 300gg–91 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (7), a child who is an inmate of a public institution or a patient in an institution for mental diseases; or

children.label: B
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (6), a child who is a member of a family that is eligible for health benefits coverage under a State health benefits plan on the basis of a family member’s employment with a public agency in the State.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 3 Special rule
children.text: A child shall not be considered to be described in paragraph (1)(C) notwithstanding that the child is covered under a health insurance coverage program that has been in operation since before , and that is offered by a State which receives no Federal funds for the program’s operation.July 1, 1997

children.heading: Medicaid applicable income level
children.label: 4 Medicaid applicable income level
children.text: The term “medicaid applicable income level” means, with respect to a child, the effective income level (expressed as a percent of the poverty line) that has been specified under the State plan under subchapter XIX (including under a waiver authorized by the Secretary or under ), as of , for the child to be eligible for medical assistance under section 1396a()(2) or 1396d(n)(2) of this title (as selected by a State) for the age of such child.section 1396a(r)(2) of this titleMarch 31, 1997l

children.heading: Option for States with a separate CHIP program to provide dental-only supplemental coverage
children.label: 5 Option for States with a separate CHIP program to provide dental-only supplemental coverage
i
children.text: dental coverage consistent with the requirements of subsection (c)(6) of ; orsection 1397cc of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: cost-sharing protection for dental coverage consistent with such requirements and the requirements of subsection (e)(3)(B) of such section.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: A State may limit the application of a waiver of paragraph (1)(C) to children whose family income does not exceed a level specified by the State, so long as the level so specified does not exceed the maximum income level otherwise established for other children under the State child health plan.

children.label: I
children.text: has the highest income eligibility standard permitted under this subchapter (or a waiver) as of ;January 1, 2009

children.label: II
children.text: does not limit the acceptance of applications for children or impose any numerical limitation, waiting list, or similar limitation on the eligibility of such children for child health assistance under such State plan; and

children.label: III
children.text: provides benefits to all children in the State who apply for and meet eligibility standards.

children.heading: No more favorable treatment
children.label: ii No more favorable treatment
children.text: The State child health plan may not provide more favorable dental coverage or cost-sharing protection for dental coverage to children provided dental-only supplemental coverage under this paragraph than the dental coverage and cost-sharing protection for dental coverage provided to targeted low-income children who are eligible for the full range of child health assistance provided under the State child health plan.

children.heading: Exceptions to exclusion of children of employees of a public agency in the State
children.label: 6 Exceptions to exclusion of children of employees of a public agency in the State
i
children.text: the public agency that employs a member of the child’s family to which such paragraph applies satisfies subparagraph (B); or

children.label: ii
children.text: subparagraph (C) applies to such child.

children.heading: Maintenance of effort with respect to agency contribution for family coverage
children.label: B Maintenance of effort with respect to agency contribution for family coverage
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(i), a public agency satisfies this subparagraph if the amount of annual agency expenditures made on behalf of employees enrolled in health coverage paid for by the agency that includes dependent coverage for the most recent State fiscal year is not less than the amount of such expenditures made by the agency for the 1997 State fiscal year, increased by the percentage increase in the medical care expenditure category of the Consumer Price Index for All-Urban Consumers (all items: U.S. City Average) for such preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: Hardship exception
children.label: C Hardship exception
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii), this subparagraph applies to a child if the State determines that the annual aggregate amount of premiums and cost-sharing imposed for coverage of the family of the child would exceed 5 percent of such family’s income for the year involved.

children.heading: Exceptions to exclusion of children who are inmates of a public institution
children.label: 7 Exceptions to exclusion of children who are inmates of a public institution
children.text: In the case of a child who is an inmate of a public institution, during the 30 days prior to the release of the child from such institution the child shall not be considered to be described in paragraph (2)(A) with respect to the screenings, diagnostic services, referrals, and case management services otherwise covered under the State child health plan (or waiver of such plan) that the State is required to provide under . At the option of the State, a child who is an inmate of a public institution shall not be considered to be described in paragraph (2)(A) during the period that the child is an inmate of such institution pending disposition of charges.section 1397bb(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Child
children.label: 1 Child
children.text: The term “child” means an individual under 19 years of age.

children.heading: Creditable health coverage
children.label: 2 Creditable health coverage
children.text: The term “creditable health coverage” has the meaning given the term “creditable coverage” under section 2701(c) of the Public Health Service Act () and includes coverage that meets the requirements of provided to a targeted low-income child under this subchapter or under a waiver approved under (relating to a direct service waiver).242 U.S.C. 300gg(c)section 1397cc of this titlesection 1397ee(c)(2)(B) of this titleSee References in Text note below.2

children.heading: Group health plan; health insurance coverage; etc.
children.label: 3 Group health plan; health insurance coverage; etc.
children.text: The terms “group health plan”, “group health insurance coverage”, and “health insurance coverage” have the meanings given such terms in .section 300gg–91 of this title

children.heading: Low-income child
children.label: 4 Low-income child
children.text: The term “low-income child” means a child whose family income is at or below 200 percent of the poverty line for a family of the size involved.

children.heading: Poverty line defined
children.label: 5 Poverty line defined
children.text: The term “poverty line” has the meaning given such term in , including any revision required by such section.section 9902(2) of this title

children.heading: Preexisting condition exclusion
children.label: 6 Preexisting condition exclusion
children.text: The term “preexisting condition exclusion” has the meaning given such term in section 2701(b)(1)(A) of the Public Health Service Act ().242 U.S.C. 300gg(b)(1)(A)

children.heading: State child health plan; plan
children.label: 7 State child health plan; plan
children.text: Unless the context otherwise requires, the terms “State child health plan” and “plan” mean a State child health plan approved under .section 1397ff of this title

children.heading: Uncovered child
children.label: 8 Uncovered child
children.text: The term “uncovered child” means a child that does not have creditable health coverage.

children.label: i
children.text: is located in or near a school facility of a school district or board or of an Indian tribe or tribal organization;

children.label: ii
children.text: is organized through school, community, and health provider relationships;

children.label: iii
children.text: is administered by a sponsoring facility;

children.label: iv
children.text: provides through health professionals primary health services to children in accordance with State and local law, including laws relating to licensure and certification; and

children.label: v
children.text: satisfies such other requirements as a State may establish for the operation of such a clinic.

children.label: i
children.text: A hospital.

children.label: ii
children.text: A public health department.

children.label: iii
children.text: A community health center.

children.label: iv
children.text: A nonprofit health care agency.

children.label: v
children.text: A local educational agency (as defined under .section 7801 of title 201

children.label: vi
children.text: A program administered by the Indian Health Service or the Bureau of Indian Affairs or operated by an Indian tribe or a tribal organization.
